cover.headline_html: 3 pricing myths that kill GSA competitiveness.

myths.label: The Copy-Paste Trap
myths.myth_text: You can just copy your commercial rates onto your GSA Schedule.
myths.reality_text: GSA requires a Basis of Award — proof your Schedule price matches your best commercial customer. Copy-pasting without an audit trail triggers modification requests and delays approval.

myths.label: The Race-to-Zero
myths.myth_text: The lower your price, the better your chances of winning contracts.
myths.reality_text: Underpricing kills margins and flags proposals for compliance review. Agencies question quality on unusually low bids. Competitive pricing with clear differentiation wins.

myths.label: The Price Lock Myth
myths.myth_text: Once GSA approves your prices, they're locked in forever.
myths.reality_text: Price modifications are available anytime with proper justification. Most contractors don't realize this flexibility exists — and leave money on the table by assuming their approval price is permanent.

close.close_question: Drop the pricing myth you believed when you first started. Let's help someone else dodge it.

template_data.image_headline: PRICING MYTHS THAT KILL YOUR MARGINS

Small label bottom right: 'Vol. 04 · Pricing'. Footer text: 'Josh Ladick · GSA Focus'.
